title: Visual accessibility audit deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because The full contrast, focus, reflow, zoom, text-spacing, target-size, motion, and assistive-technology audit requires multiple settings and human review.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Visual accessibility audit: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? Accessibility is handled entirely by semantic HTML, so CSS cannot create barriers.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Test CSS-driven focus, contrast, reflow, order, motion, and visibility because presentation can create or remove access barriers.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Test CSS-driven focus, contrast, reflow, order, motion, and visibility because presentation can create or remove access barriers.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Test CSS-driven focus, contrast, reflow, order, motion, and visibility because presentation can create or remove access barriers.
Accessibility is handled entirely by semantic HTML, so CSS cannot create barriers.

answer.explanation: Test CSS-driven focus, contrast, reflow, order, motion, and visibility because presentation can create or remove access barriers.
